_comment: Human-editable source for the Ulysses layer. Each entry is geocoded by helpers/geocode_ulysses.py via OpenStreetMap/Nominatim and written to ulysses.geojson. To add a place: copy an entry, set 'episode', 'name', and a 'geocode' query (a real, findable Dublin address/landmark). Leave 'lat'/'lon' out and the geocoder fills + caches them; set them by hand to override. 'quote' and 'ref' (Gabler episode.line, e.g. '1.1') are optional and SHOULD be verified against the public-domain text before publishing. 'kind' is 'place' (default) or 'route'.

places.name: Martello Tower, Sandycove
places.geocode: James Joyce Tower and Museum, Sandycove, Dublin
places.gloss: Stephen Dedalus, Buck Mulligan and Haines lodge here; the novel opens on its gun-platform.
places.quote: Stately, plump Buck Mulligan came from the stairhead, bearing a bowl of lather on which a mirror and a razor lay crossed.

places.name: The Forty Foot
places.gloss: The men's bathing place below the tower where Mulligan goes to swim.

places.name: Mr Deasy's school, Dalkey
places.gloss: Stephen teaches a morning history class and is paid by the headmaster Mr Deasy.

places.name: Sandymount Strand
places.gloss: Stephen walks the tideline alone, meditating on perception and the 'ineluctable modality of the visible.'
places.quote: Ineluctable modality of the visible: at least that if no more, thought through my eyes.

places.name: 7 Eccles Street
places.gloss: Leopold and Molly Bloom's home; the Odyssey of Bloom's day begins and ends here.
places.quote: Mr Leopold Bloom ate with relish the inner organs of beasts and fowls.

places.name: St George's Church
places.geocode: St George's Church, Hardwicke Place, Dublin
places.gloss: Its bells (‘Heigho! Heigho!’) sound the hour over Bloom's breakfast.

places.name: Sweny's Pharmacy
places.gloss: Bloom buys the lemon soap that he carries through the day.

places.name: Westland Row Station
places.gloss: Formerly Westland Row; Bloom collects a letter under the name Henry Flower.

places.name: Glasnevin Cemetery
places.gloss: Paddy Dignam's funeral; Bloom rides out in the carriage and broods on death.

places.name: Freeman's Journal office
places.gloss: The newspaper offices where Bloom tries to place an ad; the episode is set in the rhetoric of headlines.

places.name: Nelson's Pillar (site)
places.geocode: General Post Office, O'Connell Street, Dublin
places.gloss: The old Pillar on Sackville (O'Connell) Street; hub of the trams in the 'Aeolus' wind imagery.

places.name: Davy Byrne's
places.gloss: Bloom lunches on a gorgonzola sandwich and a glass of burgundy in the 'moral pub.'
places.quote: He entered Davy Byrne's. Moral pub. He doesn't chat. Stands a drink now and then.

places.name: National Library of Ireland
places.geocode: National Library of Ireland, Kildare Street, Dublin
places.gloss: Stephen expounds his Hamlet theory to the librarians; Bloom passes between the doors.

places.name: Ormond Hotel
places.gloss: The barmaids Miss Douce and Miss Kennedy; music and song fill the 'Sirens' episode.

places.name: Barney Kiernan's (site)
places.gloss: The pub of the nameless narrator and the hostile Citizen; Bloom is driven out under a hail of abuse.

places.name: Star of the Sea Church, Sandymount
places.gloss: Its evening service counterpoints Gerty MacDowell on the strand below.

places.name: National Maternity Hospital, Holles Street
places.geocode: National Maternity Hospital, Holles Street, Dublin
places.gloss: Mina Purefoy gives birth; the prose gestates through the history of English styles.

places.name: Nighttown (Mabbot Street)
places.gloss: The brothel quarter (Monto); the hallucinatory 'Circe' drama. Mabbot Street is now Corporation Street/James Joyce Street.

places.name: Cabman's shelter, Butt Bridge
places.gloss: Bloom takes the exhausted Stephen for coffee in the small hours.

places.name: 7 Eccles Street (return)
places.gloss: Bloom, locked out, climbs over the area railings and lets Stephen in; the catechism of 'Ithaca.'
